title: System Model: Input–Output Description
summary: This page describes translational mechanical system elements, focusing on the linear dashpot where force is proportional to velocity via the damping coefficient B. Example 1.18 presents a translational mechanical system with a mass, spring, and dashpot, asking for the input-output relationship between applied force x(t) and mass position y(t).

figures.caption: Some elements in translational mechanical systems, including (a) a mass M, (b) a spring with stiffness K, and (c) a linear dashpot with damping coefficient B.

Mechanical system consisting of a mass M, spring K, and damper B driven by an applied force x(t) with output position y(t), shown in two physical configurations (a) and (b) and a free-body diagram (c).
